# UC-10.14.10 · SCADA HMI Tampering Indicators

## Description

Detects unauthorized screen, tag, or recipe edits on HMIs (T0831), which can mislead operators or suppress alarms during an attack.

## Value

Detects unauthorized screen, tag, or recipe edits on HMIs (T0831), which can mislead operators or suppress alarms during an attack.

## Implementation

Requires Splunk ES with OT Security Add-on parsing for vendor HMI audit trails. Align with operator procedures and shift schedules. Map findings to T0831.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_security` `sourcetype="ics:hmi"` audit logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Requires Splunk ES with OT Security Add-on parsing for vendor HMI audit trails. Align with operator procedures and shift schedules. Map findings to T0831.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ics:hmi"
| where match(action, "(?i)edit|write|delete|import|export|recipe|screen")
| lookup ot_hmi_maintenance hmi_id OUTPUT maint_window
| eval in_maint=if(_time>=mvindex(maint_window,0) AND _time<=mvindex(maint_window,1), 1, 0)
| where in_maint=0 OR isnull(maint_window)
| stats count values(object) as objects by hmi_id, user, src, action
```

Understanding this SPL

**SCADA HMI Tampering Indicators** — Detects unauthorized screen, tag, or recipe edits on HMIs (T0831), which can mislead operators or suppress alarms during an attack.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_security` `sourcetype="ics:hmi"` audit logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_security; **sourcetype**: ics:hmi. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_security, sourcetype="ics:hmi". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(action, "(?i)edit|write|delete|import|export|recipe|screen")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **in_maint** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where in_maint=0 OR isnull(maint_window)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hmi_id, user, src, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare HMI or screen-change events to the **DCS/SCADA** console’s own audit trail and a quick check with the console operator for the shift.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline of HMI changes, Table with user and station.


## SPL

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ics:hmi"
| where match(action, "(?i)edit|write|delete|import|export|recipe|screen")
| lookup ot_hmi_maintenance hmi_id OUTPUT maint_window
| eval in_maint=if(_time>=mvindex(maint_window,0) AND _time<=mvindex(maint_window,1), 1, 0)
| where in_maint=0 OR isnull(maint_window)
| stats count values(object) as objects by hmi_id, user, src, action
```
